parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: A natural-language description of the problem the user is facing, in their own words, e.g. 'my git history is a mess, how do I clean it up?' or 'how do I make a column of boxes line up horizontally'. This text is embedded and compared against every stored question.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: How many of the closest-matching questions to return. Defaults to 5.

FindSimilarQuestions.description: Semantic search over StackOverflow questions, powered by SQL Server 2025 native vector search and local Ollama embeddings. Call this whenever a user describes a problem or topic in their own words rather than giving exact keywords or a question id (e.g. 'why is my async function returning a Promise instead of a value', 'how to undo a commit'). Matching is by meaning, so relevant questions come back even with no shared keywords. Returns the closest-matching questions ranked by cosine distance (smaller = better), with title, tags, score, view count, and answer count.

Questions.description: StackOverflow questions: id, title, tags, score, view count, answer count, and creation date. Use this for exact lookups and structured filters (by id, by tag substring, by score or view-count thresholds, by date). For fuzzy 'find questions like…' requests, use FindSimilarQuestions instead.

Users.description: StackOverflow users: id, display name, reputation, location, and about-me. Join from a question's OwnerUserId to see who asked it, or filter by reputation/location.
